title: Excise duty rates - Canada.ca
content:
This page provides the rates of all excise duties imposed under the Excise Act on beer and under the Excise Act, 2001 on cannabis products, spirits, tobacco products, vaping products, and wine. It also provides the rates of excise duty in effect during the previous four years, where applicable.
In addition, this page lists the price payable to purchase excise stamps for cannabis, tobacco, and vaping products from the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) authorized stamp provider.
- Beer Regular rates of excise duty on beer packaged in Canada Reduced rates of excise duty on beer brewed by domestic brewers
- Regular rates of excise duty on beer packaged in Canada
- Reduced rates of excise duty on beer brewed by domestic brewers
- Cannabis products Alberta British Columbia Manitoba New Brunswick Newfoundland and Labrador Northwest Territories Nova Scotia Nunavut Ontario Prince Edward Island Quebec Saskatchewan Yukon
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Northwest Territories
- Prince Edward Island
- Spirits Excise duty on spirits Special duty on spirits
- Excise duty on spirits
- Special duty on spirits
- Tobacco products Cigarette inventory tax Excise duty on stamped tobacco products (except raw leaf tobacco) Excise duty on raw leaf tobacco Additional duty on cigars Special duty on unstamped imported manufactured tobacco delivered to a duty free shop Special duty on traveller's tobacco Special duty on unstamped tobacco products manufactured in Canada and exported Special duty on stamped tobacco products manufactured in Canada and exported
- Cigarette inventory tax
- Excise duty on stamped tobacco products (except raw leaf tobacco)
- Excise duty on raw leaf tobacco
- Additional duty on cigars
- Special duty on unstamped imported manufactured tobacco delivered to a duty free shop
- Special duty on traveller's tobacco
- Special duty on unstamped tobacco products manufactured in Canada and exported
- Special duty on stamped tobacco products manufactured in Canada and exported
- Wine Excise duty on wine
- Price for excise stamps

## Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer
On April 1, 2026, the Government of Canada announced a proposed amendment to the Excise Act .
For details, go to Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer .
Annual rate adjustments for beer
The rates of excise duty on beer are adjusted annually on April 1 based on changes to the Consumer Price Index.
Beer includes malt liquor.
Beer that contains not more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume (that is, non-alcoholic beer) is not subject to excise duty.

### Regular rates of excise duty on beer packaged in Canada
The regular rates of excise duty are determined in accordance with Part II of the Schedule to the Excise Act . They apply to the following:
- beer packaged in Canada in excess of 75,000 hectolitres in a calendar year
- beer packaged in Canada that is either: imported beer produced from imported wort
- produced from imported wort
All rates apply per hectolitre of beer.
Rate effective April 1, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Rate effective April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023
Beer containing not more than 1.2% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
Beer containing more than 1.2% but not more than 2.5% absolute ethyl    alcohol by volume
Beer containing more than 2.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume

### Reduced rates of excise duty on beer brewed by domestic brewers

#### Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer
On April 1, 2026, the Government of Canada announced a proposed amendment to the Excise Act .
For details, go to Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer .
The reduced rates of excise duty are determined in accordance with Part II.1 of the Schedule to the Excise Act . They apply to the first 75,000 hectolitres of beer brewed in Canada (production volume limit) each calendar year by a licensed brewer and any person related to or associated with that brewer.
All rates apply per hectolitre of beer.
Annual production volume increments
Rate effective April 1, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Rate effective April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023
0 to 2,000 hectolitres
2,001 to 5,000 hectolitres
5,001 to 15,000 hectolitres
15,001 to 50,000 hectolitres
50,001 to 75,000 hectolitres

For information on how to calculate the cannabis duty, the additional cannabis duty and the adjustment to the additional cannabis duty payable on cannabis products produced in Canada, refer to Memorandum EDM6-3, Imposition and payment of duty on cannabis products . The adjustment to the additional cannabis duty must be calculated for Alberta, Nunavut, Ontario, and Saskatchewan.
The rates of flat-rate cannabis duty and ad valorem cannabis duty on cannabis products are set out, respectively, in sections 1 and 2 of Schedule 7 to the Excise Act, 2001 .
Only the greater of the flat-rate cannabis duty and the ad valorem cannabis duty is payable. If the flat-rate and ad valorem cannabis duty amounts are equal, then the flat-rate cannabis duty is payable.
Flat-rate cannabis duty
Ad valorem cannabis duty
Dried/fresh cannabis – Flowering material
$0.25 per gram of flowering material
2.5% of the dutiable amount for the cannabis product
Dried/fresh cannabis – Non-flowering material
$0.075 per gram of non-flowering material
2.5% of the dutiable amount for the cannabis product
$0.25 per viable seed
2.5% of the dutiable amount for the cannabis product
$0.25 per vegetative cannabis plant
2.5% of the dutiable amount for the cannabis product
Cannabis extracts (including cannabis oil)
$0.0025 per milligram of total THC
$0.0025 per milligram of total THC
$0.0025 per milligram of total THC
The rates of additional cannabis duty on cannabis products for Alberta are set out in Schedule 8 to the Excise Duties on Cannabis Regulations . More specifically, the flat-rate additional cannabis duty and adjustment rates are set out, respectively, in sections 1 and 5 and the ad valorem additional cannabis duty and adjustment rates are set out, respectively, in sections 2 and 6 of the Schedule.
Only the greater of the flat-rate additional cannabis duty and the ad valorem additional cannabis duty, plus the adjustment, is payable. If the flat-rate and ad valorem additional cannabis duty amounts are equal, then the flat-rate additional cannabis duty, plus the adjustment, is payable.
Flat-rate additional cannabis duty
Ad valorem additional cannabis duty
Adjustment to the additional cannabis duty

$0.75 per gram of flowering material
7.5% of the dutiable amount for the cannabis product
16.8% of the base amount or the dutiable amount

## Proposed extension of rate cap for spirits
On April 1, 2026, the Government of Canada announced a proposed amendment to the Excise Act, 2001 . Consequently, the annual adjustment to the rates of excise duty on spirits, which is usually based on changes to the Consumer Price Index, would remain capped at 2% for two years.
This proposed amendment would not change the rates in effect as of April 1, 2026, which had already been adjusted by 2%.
Annual rate adjustments for spirits
The rates of excise duty on spirits are adjusted annually on April 1 based on changes to the Consumer Price Index.
This annual adjustment does not apply to the rate of special duty on spirits.

### Excise duty on spirits
Spirits that contain not more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume (that is, non-alcoholic spirits) are not subject to excise duty.
The rates of excise duty on spirits are determined in accordance with Schedule 4 to the Excise Act, 2001 and apply:
- per litre of spirits for spirits containing not more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
- per litre of absolute ethyl alcohol for spirits containing more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
Rate effective April 1, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Rate effective April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023
Spirits containing not more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
Spirits containing more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume

### Special duty on spirits
The rate of special duty on spirits delivered to or imported by a licensed user is $0.12 per litre of absolute ethyl alcohol. This rate is set out in Schedule 5 to the Excise Act, 2001 and has been in effect since July 1, 2003.
Annual rate adjustments for certain tobacco products
The rates of excise duty and special duty on certain tobacco products are adjusted annually on April 1 based on changes to the Consumer Price Index.
This annual adjustment does not apply to the rate of excise duty on raw leaf tobacco or to the rates of special duty on stamped tobacco products manufactured in Canada and exported.

### Cigarette inventory tax
An annual cigarette inventory tax applies to cigarettes held in inventory at 12:01 am on April 1 . If you own cigarettes that will be for sale in the ordinary course of your business, you must determine your inventory of cigarettes at the beginning of the day on April 1 in order to calculate the amount of cigarette inventory tax payable.
For more information on the cigarette inventory tax payable on cigarettes held in inventory at 12:01 am on April 1 , 2026, refer to Notice EDN106, Cigarette inventory tax on April 1, 2026 .

### Excise duty on stamped tobacco products (except raw leaf tobacco)
The rates of excise duty on stamped tobacco products (except raw leaf tobacco) are determined in accordance with sections 1 to 4 of Schedule 1 to the Excise Act, 2001 and apply:
- per 5 cigarettes or fraction of 5 cigarettes contained in any package for cigarettes
- per tobacco stick for tobacco sticks
- per 50 grams or fraction of 50 grams contained in a package for manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks
- per 1,000 cigars for cigars
Rate effective April 1, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Rate effective April 17, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Rate effective April 1 to April 16, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023
Manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks

### Excise duty on raw leaf tobacco
The rate of excise duty on raw leaf tobacco is $1.572 per kilogram. This rate is set out in section 5 of Schedule 1 to the Excise Act, 2001 and has been in effect since July 1, 2003.

### Additional duty on cigars
The rates of additional duty on cigars are determined in accordance with Schedule 2 to the Excise Act, 2001 .

### Special duty on unstamped imported manufactured tobacco delivered to a duty free shop
The rates of special duty on unstamped imported manufactured tobacco delivered to a duty free shop are determined in accordance with section 1 of Schedule 3 to the Excise Act, 2001 and apply:
- per cigarette for cigarettes
- per tobacco stick for tobacco sticks
- per 50 grams or fraction of 50 grams contained in a package for manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks

Schedule 8 to the Excise Act, 2001 sets out the rates of vaping duty and additional vaping duty on vaping products. As a result of amendments to the Act that were included in Bill C-69, Budget Implementation Act, 2024, No. 1 , which received royal assent on June 20 , 2024, the rates of vaping duty and additional vaping duty on vaping products increased effective July 1, 2024.
The rates of vaping duty on vaping liquids are :
- $1.12 per 2 millilitres (mL) or fraction thereof for the first 10 mL of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container
- $1.12 per 10 mL or fraction thereof for amounts over the first 10 mL
The rates of vaping duty on vaping solids are :
- $1.12 per 2 grams (g) or fraction thereof for the first 10 g of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container
- $1.12 per 10 g or fraction thereof for amounts over the first 10 g
The rates of additional vaping duty in respect of vaping liquids and specified vaping provinces are :
- $1.12 per 2 mL or fraction thereof for the first 10 mL of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container
- $1.12 per 10 mL or fraction thereof for amounts over the first 10 mL
The rates of additional vaping duty in respect of vaping solids and specified vaping provinces are:
- $1.12 per 2 g or fraction thereof for the first 10 g of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container
- $1.12 per 10 g or fraction thereof for amounts over the first 10 g
For more information about the additional vaping duty imposed in respect of specified vaping provinces under the coordinated vaping duty system, refer to Notice EDN95, Coordinated vaping duty system , Notice EDN99, Additional specified vaping provinces effective November 6, 2024 , and Notice EDN107, Additional specified vaping province effective February 26, 2026 .
Prior to July 1, 2024, the rates of vaping duty were:
- $1 per 2 mL or g or fraction thereof for the first 10 mL or g of vaping substance in the vaping device or immediate container
- $1 per 10 mL or g or fraction thereof for amounts over the first 10 mL or g

## Proposed extension of rate cap for wine
On April 1, 2026, the Government of Canada announced a proposed amendment to the Excise Act, 2001 . Consequently, the annual adjustment to the rates of excise duty on wine, which is usually based on changes to the Consumer Price Index, would remain capped at 2% for two years.
This proposed amendment would not change the rates in effect as of April 1, 2026, which had already been adjusted by 2%.
Annual rate adjustments for wine
The rates of excise duty on wine are adjusted annually on April 1 based on changes to the Consumer Price Index.
100% Canadian wine made from honey or apples is not subject to excise duty.
Wine that contains not more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume (that is, non-alcoholic wine) is also not subject to excise duty.
The rates of excise duty on wine are determined in accordance with Schedule 6 to the Excise Act, 2001 and apply per litre of wine.
Rate effective April 1, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Rate effective April 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Rate effective April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2024
Rate effective April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023
Wine containing not more than 1.2% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
Wine containing more than 1.2% but not more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by    volume
Wine containing more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume

## Price for excise stamps
Excise stamps are distributed through a secure process administered by the CRA, and the price of the stamp is governed by the public tendering process. This price is charged and collected by the CRA authorized stamp provider.
Under the current contract, the price of an excise stamp for cannabis, tobacco, or vaping products is:
- $0.0070149 for the period from October 1, 2025 to September 30, 2026
- $0.0071552 for the period from October 1, 2026 to September 30, 2027
- $0.0072983 for the period from October 1, 2027 to September 30, 2028
- $0.0074443 for the period from October 1, 2028 to September 30, 2029
- $0.0075932 for the period from October 1, 2029 to September 30, 2030
- $0.0077451 for the period from October 1, 2030 to September 30, 2031
- $0.0079000 for the period from October 1, 2031 to September 30, 2032
- $0.0080580 for the period from October 1, 2032 to September 30, 2033
- $0.0082192 for the period from October 1, 2033 to September 30, 2034
All applicable taxes and delivery charges are extra.
